session_id: operator-dispatched ('let's do the live read'): pfSense check #3 gateway-report-vs-path Phase 2
preceded_by: Phase 1 core committed (6fe3438). Operator named the target explicitly and directed the witness-custody model before the read.

operator_directed_revision.what: Witness-custody layer correction. Phase 1 first modeled the witness as pfSense's CLASSIFIED gateway status (online/down/loss/delay via return_gateways_status/PHP). The operator corrected the layer: the RAW dpinger socket is the first-class witness; pfSense's classification is a second-order projection over the same daemon/config state, admissible only LATER as a separate 'pfSense reports status X' comparator receipt, never the base specimen.
operator_directed_revision.effect: Core rewritten to a dpinger-metrics-first custody model. socket_absent / socket_unreadable / unknown_custody are first-class cannot_testify refusals (a mute/absent daemon is never 'gateway down'). The ONLY thing derived from raw metrics is loss<100 (the daemon is getting replies) — the minimal signal, not a health grade. No reimplementation of pfSense UI thresholds as authority.

target_authorization: Operator-named: host 192.168.68.1, user admin, key ~/.ssh/nq_pfsense_probe (dedicated read-only probe key), vantage sushi-k-lan. The dedicated key/target was recovered from prior lease-presence runs/ then explicitly confirmed by the operator.
read_scope: Read-only. One SSH login: `ls /var/run/dpinger_*.sock` + `nc -U` each status socket (the same surface pfSense's own status page reads). NO PHP, NO pfctl, NO service control, NO gateway reload/refresh, NO config write. A raw-capture discovery read preceded parser-building (operator-mandated: build the parser against the observed packet, not a guessed shape).

captured_shape.box: pfSense 2.8.1-RELEASE, one WAN_DHCP gateway, WAN over CGNAT (100.64.0.0/10)

verdict_ladder: cannot_testify_dpinger_socket_absent / _unreadable / unknown_custody (lost witness custody — not down)
cannot_testify_no_path_basis (dpinger testifies, no independent probe attempted)
corroborated_by_path (dpinger reaches monitor AND every attempted probe reached)
path_ambiguous (the specimen: ANY disagreement among dpinger-reach and the probes; never WAN-down)
egress_trouble_not_wan_down (dpinger not reaching monitor AND all probes failed; stronger evidence, still not WAN-down)
probes: Two SEPARATE observations (operator: more observations, stricter claim boundaries): dpinger monitor IP (role monitor_target) + fixed egress anchor 1.1.1.1 (role egress_anchor). ICMP-first; TCP fallback ONLY when ICMP cannot execute. The monitor probe runs only when the report yields a monitor IP (no invented target).

live_observations.corroborated_by_path: Real WAN_DHCP: dpinger reaching its monitor (loss 0%, rtt ~2.7ms) AND both ICMP probes (monitor IP + 1.1.1.1) reached from sushi-k-lan.
live_observations.cannot_testify_dpinger_socket_absent: Asking for a non-existent gateway (--gateway WAN2_NONEXISTENT) yielded the custody refusal EVEN THOUGH the egress anchor 1.1.1.1 was reachable. A reachable anchor does not lift a gateway whose socket we cannot find — the refusal is the product.

verification: set -o pipefail; cargo test -p nq-monitor -> EXIT=0 (bare run, exit code is the verdict), 0 compiler warnings. Core 15 fixtures + transport parser/sink fixtures. Build clean after removing unused HttpGet variant + DEFAULT_EGRESS_ANCHOR const (YAGNI).
privacy: Receipts persisted append-only under gitignored runs/gateway-path/<stamp>/WAN_DHCP.json. Real WAN/monitor IPs (CGNAT) NOT committed; source/doc fixtures use TEST-NET 198.51.100/24. Paranoia scan: scrubbed two real CGNAT IPs from new-file comments before commit; re-scan of tracked source+docs for 100.6.75.*/192.168.68.* = clean.
fences_honored: No push (6fe3438 + 80295fb + this governor commit local-only). No PHP comparator (deferred second-order witness). No external/off-LAN vantage. No scheduler, no dashboard authority, no Lane B, no DB write (active-witness lane, receipt-only). No check #1 (declared-deny gold finding). No SNMP, no Kea.
